summaries: Between 09:22 and 13:45 UTC on 09 Apr 2026, customers running {service} on Amazon EKS in the us-east-1 Region experienced write failures on volume {volume}. The persistent volume {pvc_name} reached {disk_pct}% capacity ({disk_used} of {disk_total} used). All read operations continued normally, but write attempts returned ENOSPC errors. Kubelet on node {node} evicted pod {pod} after the DiskPressure condition was set. The issue was resolved by expanding the volume at 13:45 UTC.

Tracking ID: AZ-DK6-20260411 — Between approximately 14:00 and 18:30 UTC on 11 Apr 2026, a subset of customers using Azure Kubernetes Service in the North Europe region experienced write errors for {service}. Persistent volume {pvc_name} reached {disk_pct}% utilization ({disk_used} of {disk_total}). Write operations failed with ENOSPC while read operations remained unaffected. Pod {pod} was evicted by the Kubelet DiskPressure eviction manager at 16:45 UTC. Volume expansion was completed at 18:30 UTC.

11:35 AM PDT — We are investigating write failures for {service} in the us-central1 region. Volume {volume} on node {node} has reached {disk_pct}% capacity ({disk_used} of {disk_total} used). The Kubernetes Kubelet has flagged a DiskPressure condition and begun evicting pods. Read operations are unaffected. Our team is working to expand the volume or free capacity.

Google Cloud Status — The issue with Google Kubernetes Engine affecting {service} in us-east4 has been resolved as of 15:10 US/Pacific. Between 10:30 and 15:10 US/Pacific, persistent volume {pvc_name} reached {disk_pct}% capacity. ENOSPC errors prevented write operations for {service} while reads continued normally. Pod {pod} on node {node} was evicted due to the DiskPressure node condition. The issue was resolved by expanding the persistent disk.

Tracking ID: 8_Q3-LM8 — Summary of impact: Between approximately 03:15 and 07:40 UTC on 12 Apr 2026, customers running {service} on Azure Container Apps in West US 2 experienced write failures. Volume {volume} reached {disk_used} of {disk_total} ({disk_pct}% utilized). Log rotation had stalled, causing log files to accumulate at {growth_rate}/hour. Read operations were unaffected throughout. The issue was mitigated at 07:40 UTC by clearing accumulated log files and expanding the volume.

04/08/2026 EKS Volume Full Incident — Resolved — Between 18:00 and 22:15 PDT, {service} pods on node {node} in the us-west-2 Region experienced ENOSPC write errors after volume {volume} reached {disk_pct}% capacity ({disk_used} of {disk_total} used). Kubelet evicted pod {pod} after the DiskPressure eviction threshold was crossed. Read operations remained unaffected throughout. Volume expansion at 22:15 PDT resolved the issue.

Between 07:00 and 11:30 UTC on 07 Apr 2026, customers running {service} in Google Cloud region europe-west2 experienced write failures. The persistent volume {pvc_name} attached to pod {pod} reached {disk_used} used of {disk_total} total ({disk_pct}%). All write operations failed with ENOSPC while read operations proceeded normally. Node {node} entered DiskPressure state and the Kubelet evicted lower-priority workloads. The volume was expanded to resolve the issue at 11:30 UTC.

We experienced write errors for {service} in the ap-northeast-1 Region between 16:45 and 21:00 UTC. Persistent volume {pvc_name} on node {node} reached {disk_pct}% utilization ({disk_used} of {disk_total} used). Log files generated by {service} had been accumulating at {growth_rate}/hour without rotation. Write failures began when the volume reached capacity. Read operations were unaffected throughout the incident window.

Tracking ID: AZ-KS5-20260413 — Customers using Azure Kubernetes Service in the UK West region reported write failures for {service} between 01:00 and 05:30 UTC on 13 Apr 2026. Volume {volume} reached {disk_pct}% capacity. ENOSPC errors were returned for all write operations. The DiskPressure node condition triggered Kubelet eviction of pod {pod} at 02:45 UTC. Read requests remained available throughout. Volume expansion was completed at 05:30 UTC.

09:40 AM PDT — Amazon ECS tasks running {service} in the eu-west-3 Region are experiencing write failures. Attached volume {volume} has reached {disk_used} of {disk_total} ({disk_pct}% utilized). All attempts to write new records or flush buffers are returning ENOSPC. Read operations are functioning normally. We are working to expand the volume or remove stale data. Reads are unaffected.

Google Cloud Status Update — 16:45 US/Pacific — We are investigating write failures for {service} in GKE clusters in asia-southeast1. Persistent volume {pvc_name} has reached {disk_pct}% capacity ({disk_used} of {disk_total}). The Kubelet on node {node} has set the DiskPressure condition and evicted pod {pod}. Read operations are unaffected. Disk usage is growing at approximately {growth_rate}/hour. Engineers are working to expand the persistent disk.

Between 12:30 and 17:00 UTC on 13 Apr 2026, customers running {service} on Amazon EKS in the sa-east-1 Region experienced ENOSPC write errors on volume {volume}. The volume reached {disk_used} of {disk_total} ({disk_pct}% utilized). Log rotation had stalled, causing log accumulation at {growth_rate}/hour. Read operations continued normally. Pod {pod} on node {node} was evicted by Kubelet after the DiskPressure condition was triggered.

Tracking ID: GCP-INC-20260408-004 — Between 20:00 UTC on 08 Apr and 02:15 UTC on 09 Apr 2026, GKE clusters in northamerica-northeast1 running {service} experienced write failures. Persistent volume {pvc_name} reached {disk_pct}% capacity ({disk_used} of {disk_total} used). ENOSPC errors prevented writes while reads remained operational. Disk growth rate of {growth_rate}/hour was driven by log accumulation. Node {node} entered DiskPressure state.

04/14/2026 Volume Capacity Incident — Mitigated — Tracking ID: AZ-WE5-20260414 — Between 08:00 and 12:45 UTC, {service} on Azure Kubernetes Service in the Southeast Asia region experienced write failures after persistent volume {pvc_name} reached {disk_used} of {disk_total} capacity ({disk_pct}%). Read operations were unaffected. Pod {pod} was evicted by Kubelet due to the DiskPressure node condition at 09:30 UTC. Volume expansion was completed at 12:45 UTC.

06:22 PM PDT — We have resolved the write failure affecting {service} in the ap-southeast-2 Region. Between 01:45 and 06:22 PM PDT, volume {volume} was at {disk_pct}% capacity ({disk_used} of {disk_total}). ENOSPC errors prevented all write operations. Read operations remained fully functional throughout. Pod {pod} on node {node} was evicted after the DiskPressure threshold was crossed. Volume expansion and log cleanup at 06:22 PM PDT restored normal write operations.

Between 05:00 and 09:45 UTC on 15 Apr 2026, customers in the Google Cloud europe-west6 region running {service} experienced write failures. The persistent disk {pvc_name} attached to pod {pod} filled to {disk_used} of {disk_total} ({disk_pct}%). Log files were accumulating at {growth_rate}/hour due to a misconfigured rotation policy. All read operations continued normally. Node {node} entered DiskPressure state and evicted the pod. The issue was resolved by expanding the disk.

Tracking ID: AZ-NC9-20260416 — Summary of impact: Between approximately 11:30 UTC and 16:00 UTC on 16 Apr 2026, Azure Kubernetes Service customers in the France Central region running {service} experienced ENOSPC write errors. Volume {volume} reached {disk_pct}% capacity ({disk_used} of {disk_total} used). Read operations were unaffected. The DiskPressure eviction manager evicted pod {pod} at 13:00 UTC. Volume expansion at 16:00 UTC resolved the issue.

02:15 AM PDT — Amazon EKS pods running {service} in the eu-north-1 Region are experiencing write failures on persistent volume {pvc_name}. Volume usage has reached {disk_used} of {disk_total} ({disk_pct}%). Log accumulation at {growth_rate}/hour has consumed available space. Write operations are returning ENOSPC while reads are unaffected. Node {node} has entered DiskPressure state. We are working to expand the volume.

Google Cloud Status — Resolved — 19:30 US/Pacific — The incident affecting {service} in GKE clusters in us-west3 has been resolved. Between 13:00 and 19:30 US/Pacific, persistent volume {pvc_name} filled to {disk_pct}% capacity ({disk_used} of {disk_total}). ENOSPC errors blocked all write operations while reads continued normally. Pod {pod} on node {node} was evicted due to DiskPressure. Volume expansion resolved write errors within minutes of completion.

Between 10:00 UTC on 05 Apr and 03:30 UTC on 06 Apr 2026, customers running {service} on Amazon EKS in the me-central-1 Region experienced write failures. Volume {volume} on node {node} reached {disk_used} of {disk_total} ({disk_pct}% utilized). Log files had been accumulating without rotation at {growth_rate}/hour for an extended period. Write operations returned ENOSPC while all read operations remained functional. Kubelet evicted pod {pod} after DiskPressure threshold was crossed.
